instructionsection.title: STNT1H (scalar plus scalar, strided registers) -- A64
instructionsection.heading: STNT1H (scalar plus scalar, strided registers)

brief.para: Contiguous store non-temporal of halfwords from multiple strided vectors (scalar index)

authored.para: This instruction performs a contiguous non-temporal store of halfwords from elements of two or four strided vector registers to the memory address generated by a 64-bit scalar base and scalar index that is added to the base address. After each element access the index value is incremented, but the index register is not updated.
Inactive elements are not written to memory.
A non-temporal store is a hint to the system that this data is unlikely to be referenced again soon.

uses_dit.condition: FEAT_SVE2 is implemented or FEAT_SME is implemented

operationalnote_content.para: This instruction is a data-independent-time instruction as described in About PSTATE.DIT.

a.hover: For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register Z0-Z7 or Z16-Z23 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'0':Zt".
For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the second scalable vector register Z8-Z15 or Z24-Z31 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'1':Zt".
Is the name of the governing scalable predicate register PN8-PN15, with predicate-as-counter encoding, encoded in the "PNg" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose offset register, encoded in the "Rm" field.

ps.name: A64.sme.mortlach_multi_mem_nctg.mortlach_multi2_cst_cstnt_ss_nctg.stnt1h_mzx_p_br_2x8

a.hover: For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register Z0-Z3 or Z16-Z19 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'00':Zt".
For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the second scalable vector register Z4-Z7 or Z20-Z23 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'01':Zt".
Is the name of the third scalable vector register Z8-Z11 or Z24-Z27 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'10':Zt".
Is the name of the fourth scalable vector register Z12-Z15 or Z28-Z31 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'11':Zt".
Is the name of the governing scalable predicate register PN8-PN15, with predicate-as-counter encoding, encoded in the "PNg" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose offset register, encoded in the "Rm" field.

ps.name: A64.sme.mortlach_multi_mem_nctg.mortlach_multi4_cst_cstnt_ss_nctg.stnt1h_mzx_p_br_4x4

intro.para: For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register Z0-Z7 or Z16-Z23 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'0':Zt".
For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register Z0-Z3 or Z16-Z19 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'00':Zt".
For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the second scalable vector register Z8-Z15 or Z24-Z31 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'1':Zt".
For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the second scalable vector register Z4-Z7 or Z20-Z23 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'01':Zt".
Is the name of the governing scalable predicate register PN8-PN15, with predicate-as-counter encoding, encoded in the "PNg" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose offset register, encoded in the "Rm" field.
Is the name of the third scalable vector register Z8-Z11 or Z24-Z27 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'10':Zt".
Is the name of the fourth scalable vector register Z12-Z15 or Z28-Z31 to be transferred, encoded as "T:'11':Zt".

ps.name: A64.sme.mortlach_multi_mem_nctg.mortlach_multi2_cst_cstnt_ss_nctg.stnt1h_mzx_p_br_2x8
